summary: First full test run of Taara & Cookie's Day Out app. Backend: 12/13 tests passed (the 1 failure is a test ordering issue, not a bug). Frontend: All screens tested and working. Auth flow via localStorage/AsyncStorage works correctly. All major user flows verified.

minor.endpoint: POST /api/stops/{stop_id}/unlock with wrong password on already-unlocked stop
minor.issue: Backend correctly returns 200 for already-unlocked stop (not a bug), but test ordering caused test_unlock_wrong_password to fail since stop was already unlocked by prior test. This is a test design issue, not a backend bug.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: useNativeDriver not supported in web preview. Falls back to JS-based animation. Purely cosmetic, animations still work.

critical_code_review_comments: The /api/auth/me endpoint and all authenticated routes correctly exclude _id from MongoDB responses.
The AuthContext uses AsyncStorage (localStorage on web) for token storage - cookie-based testing won't work, must use localStorage.
Sequential stop unlocking logic is properly implemented (previous stop must be done).
Photo upload stores base64 in MongoDB - may have performance/size concerns at scale but acceptable for MVP.

success_rate.backend: 92% (12/13 tests passed, 1 is a test ordering issue not a code bug)

seed_data_creation: Used pre-seeded demo user (taara@demo.com) and session sess_5704d6ebc25c4924 with 4 stops
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth uses AsyncStorage (localStorage on web). To test authenticated routes in browser, set localStorage key 'session_token' to 'test_session_demo_taara_cookie' before navigating. Stop 1 (Meeting point) is already done with 5-star rating and TEST_comment. Stop 2 (Dinner) was unlocked with password 'ramen' during this test run. Stops 3 and 4 remain locked.
